title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · Graph Forensics
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective repository-state and revision-query proof; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in repository-state and revision-query proof and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G58 is a debug in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G58 is a debug in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A clean working tree is a complete forensic baseline.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-q6swkw" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-q6swkw
# scenario-key: trace-q6swkw probe-wkws6q boundary-swkwq6 owner-q6swkwx fault-q6swkwz
# before-ref: main=a11ceq6 candidate=b00b1kw
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11ceq6 candidate=c0ffeew
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
